name: agent-behavior-decomposition
phase: Restructure PM + tech-lead reviewers around named-behavior catalog

milestones.name: Catalog convention scaffold
milestones.outcome: shield/agents/behaviors/ exists as a documented, lintable catalog ready for behavior files
exit_criteria: shield/agents/behaviors/ directory exists in the repo
shield/agents/behaviors/README.md documents file-naming, frontmatter shape, body schema
CLAUDE.md gains an 'Agent Behaviors Catalog' section pointing at the convention
shield/scripts/verify-behavior-references.sh exits 0 on main and errors when an agent.md references a non-existent behavior file
Pre-commit hook in .pre-commit-config.yaml runs the verify script
Zero behavior change for any existing reviewer agent (verified by running prd-review against a fixture pre/post)

milestones.name: PM reviewer composed from behavior catalog
milestones.outcome: shield:product-manager-reviewer in PRD-Review mode walks 9 behavior files from the catalog and emits the new envelope; other 3 modes unchanged
exit_criteria: Baseline distinct-finding-count captured against all 4 PRD-review test fixtures on main; recorded in shield/evals/agent-decomposition/baseline.json

9 PM behavior files exist in shield/agents/behaviors/ (problem-clarity, scope-discipline, measurable-success, raci-and-approvals, compliance-posture, gtm-comms, support-readiness, strategic-timing, risk-articulation)
shield/agents/product-manager-reviewer.md has a '## Behaviors (PRD-Review mode)' section with the order/dim table
shield/skills/general/prd-review/personas.md PM dispatch prompt references the new protocol
shield/skills/general/prd-review/scoring.md accepts both old {dim_grades:{}} and new {behaviors:[]} envelope shapes
PM1-PM11 retained in product-manager-reviewer.md under 'Legacy framework' for Research-Framing / Research-Review / Plan-Review / Standalone modes
Merge gate passes: new envelope produces >= baseline total finding count across the 4 fixtures, no fixture regresses by more than 10%

milestones.name: Tech-lead reviewer composed from behavior catalog
milestones.outcome: shield:architecture-reviewer (tech-lead) in PRD-Review mode walks ~13 behavior files covering dim 5 (NFR) and dim 6 (rollout); same envelope as PM
exit_criteria: 13 tech-lead behavior files exist in shield/agents/behaviors/ — dim 5: perf-budget, security-threat-model, rbac-matrix, accessibility, privacy-and-retention, telemetry-completeness, i18n-l10n; dim 6: flag-plan, canary-rollout, kill-switch, abort-thresholds, data-migration, backward-compatibility
shield/agents/architecture-reviewer.md has a '## Behaviors (PRD-Review mode)' section with the order/dim table
shield/skills/general/prd-review/personas.md tech-lead dispatch prompt references the new protocol
Merge gate passes for tech-lead: new envelope produces >= baseline total finding count for tech-lead-owned dims across the 4 fixtures, no fixture regresses by more than 10%
Backward-compat acceptance in scoring.md verified by running on a fixture with old envelope

stories.name: Create shield/agents/behaviors/ directory with README
stories.description: Scaffold the catalog directory and document the convention. README.md captures file-naming (kebab-case, name matches frontmatter), frontmatter shape (name only — no owned_by, no owned_dim), and body schema (description, when_active, inputs, checks-with-severity, exceptions, output_shape).
tasks: Create directory shield/agents/behaviors/
Write shield/agents/behaviors/README.md with file-naming + frontmatter + body schema sections
Include a worked example showing the behavior file shape (problem-clarity-style)
Cross-link to the catalog from shield/agents/README.md if it exists; create it if not
Add a .gitkeep if README.md doesn't suffice to track the directory
acceptance_criteria: shield/agents/behaviors/README.md exists and renders correctly in GitHub
The README explicitly states that owned_by and owned_dim do NOT live in behavior frontmatter
The README shows a complete behavior file example (frontmatter + body) that an author could copy-paste
ls shield/agents/behaviors/ shows the README
`git diff --stat` shows zero changes to any existing reviewer agent's .md file

stories.name: Add 'Agent Behaviors Catalog' section to CLAUDE.md
stories.description: Document the catalog convention at the project level so future agent authors discover it. Add a short section to CLAUDE.md under 'Key Conventions' pointing to shield/agents/behaviors/README.md as the source of truth for behavior authoring.
tasks: Edit CLAUDE.md and add a 'Agent Behaviors Catalog' subsection under 'Key Conventions'
Reference shield/agents/behaviors/README.md as the authoritative spec
Note that agent.md files are the authoritative index (which behaviors → which dim), not the behavior files themselves
Keep the section under 10 lines — CLAUDE.md should remain scannable
acceptance_criteria: CLAUDE.md contains an 'Agent Behaviors Catalog' heading under 'Key Conventions'
The section references shield/agents/behaviors/README.md by path
grep -c 'Agent Behaviors Catalog' CLAUDE.md returns 1

stories.name: Write verify-behavior-references.sh lint script + pre-commit hook
stories.description: Prevent catalog drift between agent.md indices and behavior files. Script parses each shield/agents/*.md for behavior file path references and asserts each path exists. Wire into pre-commit so the lint runs before each commit.
tasks: Write shield/scripts/verify-behavior-references.sh — grep agent.md files for shield/agents/behaviors/*.md references and exit 1 on any missing
Make the script executable (chmod +x)
Add hook entry to .pre-commit-config.yaml that runs the script on changes to shield/agents/**/*.md
Test locally: corrupt a reference temporarily and confirm pre-commit blocks the commit; revert
acceptance_criteria: shield/scripts/verify-behavior-references.sh exists and is executable (test -x returns 0)
Running the script on main (with no behaviors referenced) exits 0
Manually-induced bad reference causes the script to exit non-zero with an actionable error message naming the missing file
.pre-commit-config.yaml includes the new hook
pre-commit run --all-files passes on the PR 0 branch

stories.name: Capture distinct-finding-count baseline against test fixtures
stories.description: Before any structural change, capture the merge-gate baseline. Run the current PM agent against the 4 PRD-review test fixtures on main, count distinct findings per fixture, sum to a total, and serialize to shield/evals/agent-decomposition/baseline.json. This is the number that the new envelope must meet or beat.
tasks: Run /prd-review against each of shield/skills/general/prd-review/test-fixtures/well-formed-standard.md, standard-with-gaps.md, lean-with-gaps.md, internal-tool.md on main
For each, count distinct findings emitted by the PM persona (de-duplicate by gap text)
Record per-fixture count, total count, and the date/commit-SHA in shield/evals/agent-decomposition/baseline.json
Document the counting methodology in shield/evals/agent-decomposition/README.md so the count is reproducible
acceptance_criteria: shield/evals/agent-decomposition/baseline.json exists with keys per-fixture, total, commit_sha, captured_at
shield/evals/agent-decomposition/README.md explains the counting rule (de-dup by gap text) precisely enough that re-running gives the same number ±2
Total >= 0 and per-fixture sum equals total
Baseline is committed to main BEFORE the PM agent restructure lands so it cannot be retroactively rewritten

stories.name: Author 9 PM behavior files in the catalog
stories.description: Write one behavior file per PM-owned rubric dimension. Each file has frontmatter (name only) + body (description, when_active, inputs, checks-with-severity, exceptions, output_shape). Content derives from the existing rubric.md eval points for dims 1, 2, 3, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12.
tasks: Author shield/agents/behaviors/problem-clarity.md (dim 1: 1a-1d eval points)
Author shield/agents/behaviors/scope-discipline.md (dim 2: 2a-2c)
Author shield/agents/behaviors/measurable-success.md (dim 3: 3a-3d)
Author shield/agents/behaviors/raci-and-approvals.md (dim 7: 7a-7d)
Author shield/agents/behaviors/compliance-posture.md (dim 8: 8a-8d)
Author shield/agents/behaviors/gtm-comms.md (dim 9: 9a-9d)
Author shield/agents/behaviors/support-readiness.md (dim 10: 10a-10d)
Author shield/agents/behaviors/strategic-timing.md (dim 11: 11a-11c)
Author shield/agents/behaviors/risk-articulation.md (dim 12: 12a-12c)
Each behavior file follows shield/agents/behaviors/README.md schema; verify with the lint script from EPIC-1-S3
acceptance_criteria: All 9 files exist at shield/agents/behaviors/<name>.md
Each file's frontmatter contains exactly {name: <kebab>} matching the filename — no owned_by, no owned_dim
Each file's body contains all 6 sections: Description, When active, Inputs, Checks (with severity per check), Exceptions, Output shape
Every check listed in the behavior file traces to a rubric.md eval point (1a, 1b, etc.) with the same severity
verify-behavior-references.sh exits 0 once EPIC-2-S3 lands

stories.name: Restructure product-manager-reviewer.md with Behaviors section
stories.description: Add a '## Behaviors (PRD-Review mode)' section to shield/agents/product-manager-reviewer.md with an order/dim/severity-max table referencing each of the 9 behavior files. Add an Execution Protocol subsection describing how the agent walks the catalog at dispatch time. Move the existing PM1-PM11 framework into a 'Legacy framework — used in Research-Framing / Research-Review / Plan-Review / Standalone modes' section so other dispatches don't break.
tasks: Edit shield/agents/product-manager-reviewer.md
Add '## Behaviors (PRD-Review mode)' section with order/dim/severity-max table for the 9 behaviors
Add '### Execution protocol' subsection: Read each behavior file in order, identify PRD inputs, evaluate checks, apply exceptions, emit per-behavior finding using output_shape
Add the JSON envelope shape spec to the protocol section
Rename existing PM1-PM11 section to 'Legacy framework — for Research-Framing / Research-Review / Plan-Review / Standalone' and note that PRD-Review mode now uses behaviors instead
Update 'Common Mistakes' table to add: forgetting to Read a behavior file; grading without applying exceptions; emitting findings without per-behavior output_shape
acceptance_criteria: shield/agents/product-manager-reviewer.md has a '## Behaviors (PRD-Review mode)' section with a 9-row table
Each row references the correct path under shield/agents/behaviors/
verify-behavior-references.sh exits 0 (all referenced behaviors exist)
PM1-PM11 content is preserved under the renamed legacy heading
Diff inspection shows no removed content from the legacy PM1-PM11 framework
Word count of the new section is between 100 and 400 words (lean index, not duplicating behavior content)

stories.name: Update prd-review personas.md and scoring.md for new envelope
stories.description: The orchestrator (shield:prd-review) must understand both the old {dim_grades:{}} envelope and the new {behaviors:[]} envelope. Update personas.md PM dispatch prompt to invoke the new behavior protocol; update scoring.md to accept both envelope shapes during the transition window.
tasks: Edit shield/skills/general/prd-review/personas.md PM dispatch prompt to reference 'walks behaviors per the agent.md table' rather than 'grades dimensions'
Edit shield/skills/general/prd-review/scoring.md to specify: orchestrator accepts envelopes with `behaviors[]` (new) or `dim_grades:{}` (old). Aggregate by `dim` field in either case.
Add a transition note to scoring.md explaining the dual acceptance window and stating that the old shape will be deprecated when all reviewer agents are migrated
Verify: dispatch the unchanged PM agent against a test fixture and confirm scoring still works (back-compat); dispatch the new PM agent and confirm scoring also works
acceptance_criteria: personas.md PM dispatch prompt references the behavior protocol
scoring.md explicitly documents acceptance of both envelope shapes
Running /prd-review on a fixture with the OLD PM agent (pre-this-PR) still produces a graded summary.md without errors
Running /prd-review on a fixture with the NEW PM agent (post-this-PR) also produces a graded summary.md without errors
The two summary.md outputs grade the same 9 dimensions (1, 2, 3, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12) — column structure unchanged

stories.name: Provide PM1-PM11 → behavior name mapper script
stories.description: Old eval cases reference PM1-PM11 check IDs. To keep them runnable without rewrites, provide a one-time mapper. Output is a small script and a mapping table.
tasks: Create shield/evals/agent-decomposition/pm-id-mapper.json mapping PM1-PM11 → corresponding behavior + check id (e.g., PM1 → problem-clarity.1a)
Write shield/scripts/translate-pm-ids.sh that reads an eval fixture and rewrites PM* references to behavior-scoped ids
Document usage in shield/evals/agent-decomposition/README.md
acceptance_criteria: shield/evals/agent-decomposition/pm-id-mapper.json exists with 11 entries (PM1 through PM11)
Each entry maps to a behavior name + check id that exists in the catalog
shield/scripts/translate-pm-ids.sh runs on a sample eval fixture and outputs valid behavior-scoped references
Documentation explains when to use the mapper (one-off migration of eval fixtures) vs. when not to (new eval cases should reference behavior ids directly)

stories.name: CI merge gate: distinct-finding-count against fixtures
stories.description: Wire the merge-gate metric into CI so PR 1 cannot land if the new PM agent regresses below the baseline. Compare new run against shield/evals/agent-decomposition/baseline.json captured in EPIC-2-S1.
tasks: Write shield/scripts/check-finding-count.sh — runs PM agent on each of the 4 fixtures, counts findings, compares to baseline.json
Add a GitHub Actions job under .github/workflows/ (or extend an existing one) that runs the check on PRs that touch shield/agents/product-manager-reviewer.md or shield/agents/behaviors/
Gate criteria: total finding count >= baseline total AND no fixture regresses by more than 10%
Emit a markdown PR comment summarizing per-fixture deltas (baseline X → new Y)
acceptance_criteria: shield/scripts/check-finding-count.sh exists and is executable
GitHub Actions workflow runs the check on PRs touching the relevant paths
The check fails (exits non-zero) when finding count regresses below the gate
The check passes on PR 1 when the new envelope meets or exceeds baseline
PR check status appears in the GitHub UI as 'Behavior decomposition / finding count'

stories.name: Author 13 tech-lead behavior files (dim 5 + dim 6)
stories.description: Write one behavior file per sub-check inside dims 5 (NFR) and 6 (rollout & ops). Each file follows the catalog schema. This is where the largest depth gain lives because each tech-lead dim today has 6-7 eval points.
tasks: Author shield/agents/behaviors/perf-budget.md (dim 5a)
Author shield/agents/behaviors/security-threat-model.md (dim 5b)
Author shield/agents/behaviors/rbac-matrix.md (dim 5c)
Author shield/agents/behaviors/accessibility.md (dim 5d)
Author shield/agents/behaviors/privacy-and-retention.md (dim 5e)
Author shield/agents/behaviors/telemetry-completeness.md (dim 5f)
Author shield/agents/behaviors/i18n-l10n.md (dim 5g)
Author shield/agents/behaviors/flag-plan.md (dim 6a)
Author shield/agents/behaviors/canary-rollout.md (dim 6b)
Author shield/agents/behaviors/kill-switch.md (dim 6c)
Author shield/agents/behaviors/abort-thresholds.md (dim 6d)
Author shield/agents/behaviors/data-migration.md (dim 6e)
Author shield/agents/behaviors/backward-compatibility.md (dim 6f)
acceptance_criteria: All 13 files exist under shield/agents/behaviors/
Each file's frontmatter contains exactly {name: <kebab>} matching the filename
Each file's body has all 6 schema sections
Each check in each behavior traces to a rubric.md eval point with matching severity
verify-behavior-references.sh exits 0 once EPIC-3-S2 lands
Dim 5b's 'security' behavior content is consistent with what a future dedicated security-reviewer agent would compose

stories.name: Restructure architecture-reviewer.md with Behaviors section
stories.description: Apply the same restructure pattern proven in EPIC-2-S3. Add a '## Behaviors (PRD-Review mode)' section referencing 13 tech-lead behaviors with dim + severity-max columns. Keep any existing legacy framework under a 'Legacy' heading.
tasks: Edit shield/agents/architecture-reviewer.md
Add '## Behaviors (PRD-Review mode)' section with 13-row table
Add '### Execution protocol' subsection (identical shape to PM's, scoped to tech-lead behaviors)
Preserve any existing non-PRD-Review-mode content under a legacy heading
Update 'Common Mistakes' if present
acceptance_criteria: shield/agents/architecture-reviewer.md has a 13-row '## Behaviors (PRD-Review mode)' table
All 13 paths exist (verify-behavior-references.sh exits 0)
Word count of the new section is between 150 and 500 words
Existing non-PRD-Review content preserved (diff inspection shows no removed content)

stories.name: Update prd-review personas.md tech-lead dispatch prompt
stories.description: Mirror the personas.md change from EPIC-2-S4 for the tech-lead persona. scoring.md does not need further changes — the dual-acceptance logic from EPIC-2-S4 already handles tech-lead's new envelope.
tasks: Edit shield/skills/general/prd-review/personas.md tech-lead (architecture-reviewer) dispatch prompt
Reference the behavior protocol like PM
Run /prd-review against well-formed-standard.md and verify summary.md still grades dims 5 + 6 correctly
acceptance_criteria: personas.md tech-lead dispatch prompt references the behavior protocol
Running /prd-review on a fixture produces a summary.md that grades dims 5 + 6 with no errors
summary.md column structure remains unchanged

stories.name: Apply CI merge gate to tech-lead
stories.description: Re-use the check-finding-count.sh script from EPIC-2-S6 with a tech-lead scoping flag. Capture a tech-lead baseline against the same 4 fixtures before this PR and gate merge on the same non-regression criteria.
tasks: Capture tech-lead-scoped baseline against the 4 PRD-review test fixtures on the post-PR-1 main; add to shield/evals/agent-decomposition/baseline.json under a tech_lead key
Parameterize check-finding-count.sh to support --persona pm|tech-lead
Extend GitHub Actions workflow to run the check on PRs touching shield/agents/architecture-reviewer.md or new tech-lead behavior files
Gate criteria identical to PM: total >= baseline, no fixture regresses >10%
acceptance_criteria: shield/evals/agent-decomposition/baseline.json contains both pm and tech_lead keys
check-finding-count.sh accepts --persona flag and runs both modes
GitHub Actions check appears for tech-lead PRs
PR 2 passes the gate

merge_gate.criteria: new envelope total finding count >= baseline total; no fixture regresses by more than 10%; stretch goal 1.3x baseline
